title: ECJ Rules Hungary's Orbán-Era LGBTQ Law Violates Human Rights
detail: The European Court of Justice ruled that Hungary's Orbán-era law restricting LGBTQ content and representation infringes on fundamental human rights. The ruling adds significant legal pressure on Magyar's incoming government as it prepares to take shape, forcing an early decision on whether to comply with EU human rights standards or continue challenging Brussels.

media.caption: ECJ rules Hungary's Orbán-era LGBTQ law infringes on human rights

title: Magyar and Fico Warn Ukraine on Druzhba Pipeline 'Blackmail'
detail: Péter Magyar and Slovak Prime Minister Robert Fico spoke by phone on April 21, jointly pressing Ukrainian President Zelensky to resume oil transit through the Druzhba pipeline, which Ukraine has disrupted. Magyar called the disruption 'blackmail' and warned that Hungary and Slovakia would not support the EU's €90 billion Ukraine aid package unless energy supplies were restored. The call also covered the rights of ethnic Hungarians living in Slovakia — a long-standing bilateral tension Magyar signaled he would raise with Bratislava — and marked his first substantive foreign policy phone diplomacy as prime minister-elect.

media.caption: Magyar and Fico jointly warn Ukraine over Druzhba oil pipeline disruption
